toolsUsed: docker compose runtime rebuild/start with explicit Flyway-v2 + MailHog overrides
docker exec psql test-user password normalization and restoration

assertions.title: Temporary credentials are not returned and unusable onboarding is rejected

steps.action: Run the Lane 02 auth/admin/OpenAPI regression pack covering onboarding, temporary-credential hardening, support reset, and public recovery parity
steps.expected: The targeted controller/service/integration coverage for temp-credential redaction and delivery-disabled onboarding remains green
steps.observed: `MIGRATION_SET=v2 mvn test -Djacoco.skip=true -pl . '-Dtest=AuthControllerIT,AuthHardeningIT,AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT,AuthTenantAuthorityIT,PasswordResetServiceTest,PasswordServiceTest,RefreshTokenServiceTest,TokenBlacklistServiceTest,AdminUserSecurityIT,AdminUserServiceTest,TenantAdminProvisioningServiceTest,OpenApiSnapshotIT'` finished with `BUILD SUCCESS`; 138 tests ran with 0 failures and 0 errors, including `TenantOnboardingControllerTest.onboardTenant_rejectsWhenCredentialDeliveryIsDisabled`, the onboarding response-redaction coverage, and the support-reset/recovery parity cases exercised by `AuthTenantAuthorityIT` and `OpenApiSnapshotIT`.

steps.action: As `uat.superadmin@example.com`, temporarily set `sendCredentials=false`, attempt an isolated tenant onboarding, restore settings, then onboard a second isolated tenant on the live compose-backed runtime
steps.expected: Delivery-disabled onboarding should fail closed without persisting rows, and a successful onboarding response should omit any temporary-password field while still reporting the admin email and credential-delivery status
steps.observed: `GET /api/v1/admin/settings` returned 200, `PUT /api/v1/admin/settings` with `sendCredentials=false` returned 200, and `POST /api/v1/superadmin/tenants/onboard` for isolated company `UTL2F...` returned 400 with message `Credential email delivery is disabled; enable erp.mail.enabled=true and erp.mail.send-credentials=true`; direct Postgres checks confirmed `companies` and `app_users` stayed at count 0 for the failed company/admin. After restoring settings with another 200 `PUT`, a second onboarding for isolated company `UTL2SED4D` returned 200 `Tenant onboarded` with data keys `companyId`, `companyCode`, `templateCode`, `accountsCreated`, `accountingPeriodId`, `adminEmail`, `credentialsEmailSent`, and `systemSettingsInitialized`; the runtime payload did not include `adminTemporaryPassword` and reported `credentialsEmailSent=true`.

steps.action: Trigger the supported root-only support reset path for `ut.taab.admin2@example.com` on tenant `UTTAAB2`
steps.expected: The live credential-reset response should remain functional for an allowed actor but must not leak temporary credentials in the API payload
steps.observed: `POST /api/v1/companies/2/support/admin-password-reset` as `uat.superadmin@example.com` returned 200 `Admin credentials reset and emailed` with data `{companyId: 2, companyCode: UTTAAB2, adminEmail: ut.taab.admin2@example.com, status: credentials-emailed}` and no `temporaryPassword` or `adminTemporaryPassword` field.

runtime.onboardingDeliveryDisabledMessage: Credential email delivery is disabled; enable erp.mail.enabled=true and erp.mail.send-credentials=true

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 138, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Must-change-password users are confined to the password-change corridor

steps.action: Run the same Lane 02 targeted regression pack that exercises must-change-password corridor enforcement and adjacent auth paths
steps.expected: Controller, filter, and password-service corridor coverage remains green
steps.observed: The same 138-test targeted Maven run completed with `BUILD SUCCESS`; it includes the corridor cases in `AuthControllerIT`, the password-change/revocation path in `PasswordServiceTest`, and the hardened auth filter behavior in `AuthHardeningIT`.

steps.action: Mark seeded user `uat.admin@example.com` as `mustChangePassword=true`, then probe allowed corridor endpoints, a denied admin surface, and a successful password change on the live runtime
steps.expected: Login, `/auth/me`, `GET /auth/profile`, and password change remain usable; normal protected admin work stays blocked until the password changes successfully
steps.observed: After the isolated flag update, `POST /api/v1/auth/login` returned 200 with `mustChangePassword=true`; `GET /api/v1/auth/me` returned 200 and still reported `mustChangePassword=true`; `GET /api/v1/auth/profile` returned 200; and `GET /api/v1/admin/users` returned 403 `Password change required before accessing this resource` with `data.code=AUTH_004`, `data.reason=PASSWORD_CHANGE_REQUIRED`, and `data.mustChangePassword=true`. `POST /api/v1/auth/password/change` then returned 200, the old bearer token was rejected on the next `/auth/me` probe with 403, a relogin with the new password returned 200 with `mustChangePassword=false`, and `GET /api/v1/admin/users` returned 200 after the password change completed.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 138, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Token digest storage uses Flyway v2 only

steps.action: Run the targeted Lane 02 pack that covers refresh-token and password-reset digest-backed storage
steps.expected: Digest-lookup, compatibility, and auth-controller coverage remains green on the Flyway-v2 test path
steps.observed: The 138-test targeted Maven run finished with `BUILD SUCCESS`; it includes `RefreshTokenServiceTest`, `PasswordResetServiceTest`, and `AuthControllerIT` coverage for digest-backed token lookup and compatibility behavior under `MIGRATION_SET=v2`.

steps.action: Inspect the committed schema path and runtime startup facts for the digest rollout
steps.expected: The schema change should exist only under `db/migration_v2`, the legacy `db/migration` path should stay untouched, and the compose-backed runtime should still be operating on schema version 158
steps.observed: `git show --name-only --format='' 1159ef54` listed `erp-domain/src/main/resources/db/migration_v2/V158__auth_token_digest_storage.sql` plus auth service/test files and no paths under `erp-domain/src/main/resources/db/migration/`. The repository currently contains `erp-domain/src/main/resources/db/migration_v2/V158__auth_token_digest_storage.sql`, and the rebuilt compose-backed runtime logs still reported `Current version of schema "public": 158` on startup.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 138, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Security events revoke prior sessions consistently

steps.action: Run the targeted Lane 02 regression pack covering logout, password change, password reset, disablement/lockout, and revocation helpers
steps.expected: Session-revocation coverage remains green across the representative security-event matrix
steps.observed: The same 138-test targeted Maven run completed with `BUILD SUCCESS`; it covers revocation-sensitive paths in `AuthControllerIT`, `PasswordServiceTest`, `PasswordResetServiceTest`, `TokenBlacklistServiceTest`, `AdminUserServiceTest`, and `AuthTenantAuthorityIT`.

steps.action: Use the supported company control-plane support-reset path as a representative live security event against an already-authenticated tenant admin session
steps.expected: After the reset, the pre-reset bearer and refresh tokens should stop authenticating while the reset response itself stays on the masked credential-delivery contract
steps.observed: `GET /api/v1/auth/me` for `ut.taab.admin2@example.com` returned 200 before the reset. `POST /api/v1/companies/2/support/admin-password-reset` as `uat.superadmin@example.com` then returned 200 `Admin credentials reset and emailed` with data `{companyId: 2, companyCode: UTTAAB2, adminEmail: ut.taab.admin2@example.com, status: credentials-emailed}`. Replaying the pre-reset bearer token against `/api/v1/auth/me` returned 403 with `reason=COMPANY_CONTEXT_AUTH_REQUIRED`, and replaying the pre-reset refresh token returned 400 with `data.code=VAL_001`.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 138, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Cross-tenant incident-response actions stay masked but functional for allowed actors

steps.action: Run the targeted Lane 02 authz/admin pack that covers foreign-target masking and allowed privileged paths
steps.expected: Tenant-boundary denial coverage and intended super-admin paths stay green
steps.observed: The same 138-test targeted Maven run finished with `BUILD SUCCESS`; it includes `AuthTenantAuthorityIT`, `AdminUserSecurityIT`, and `AdminUserServiceTest` coverage for masked foreign-target denial behavior plus allowed privileged reset paths.

steps.action: Compare foreign-target and missing-target tenant-admin responses on the live user-control and company support-reset surfaces, then re-check an allowed super-admin action
steps.expected: Tenant-admin foreign-target incident-response probes remain externally masked while the intended privileged path still succeeds
steps.observed: As `ut.taab.admin2@example.com` in `UTTAAB2`, `POST /api/v1/admin/users/3/force-reset-password` and `POST /api/v1/admin/users/999999/force-reset-password` both returned 400 `User not found` with the same controlled envelope (`data.code=VAL_001`, `data.reason=User not found`). The tenant-admin company control-plane probes `POST /api/v1/companies/1/support/admin-password-reset` and `POST /api/v1/companies/999999/support/admin-password-reset` both returned the same 403 `Access denied` contract with `data.code=AUTH_004` and `data.reason=COMPANY_CONTROL_ACCESS_DENIED`. A representative allowed privileged path still succeeded: `POST /api/v1/admin/users/3/force-reset-password` as `uat.superadmin@example.com` returned 200 `Password reset link sent`.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 138, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Recovery alias and published contract are explicit and aligned

steps.action: Run the targeted Lane 02 contract/parity pack for auth recovery surfaces
steps.expected: The retired alias, canonical forgot-password route, and OpenAPI parity checks remain green
steps.observed: The 138-test targeted Maven run completed with `BUILD SUCCESS`; it includes `AuthPasswordResetPublicContractIT`, `AuthControllerIT`, and `OpenApiSnapshotIT` coverage for the retired alias and its canonical/support reset replacement paths.

steps.action: Probe the retired super-admin forgot-password alias on the live API and cross-check the published contract surfaces
steps.expected: The runtime should return the explicit retirement contract, and OpenAPI should document the same alias/support-reset relationship while the onboarding/support docs stay aligned
steps.observed: `POST /api/v1/auth/password/forgot/superadmin` returned 410 with message `Deprecated super-admin forgot-password alias has been retired; use the supported recovery routes`, `data.canonicalPath=/api/v1/auth/password/forgot`, and `data.supportResetPath=/api/v1/companies/{id}/support/admin-password-reset`. The repository `openapi.json` documents the alias path with only a `410` response, the support-reset path uses request schema `#/components/schemas/CompanyAdminPasswordResetRequest`, and the published frontend/support notes in `docs/frontend-update-v2/lane02-recovery-contract-parity.md` and `.factory/library/frontend-handoff.md` describe the same canonical/support-reset recovery surfaces.

runtime.retiredAliasMessage: Deprecated super-admin forgot-password alias has been retired; use the supported recovery routes

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 138, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

frictions.description: Launching the delegated `user-testing-flow-validator` subagent failed immediately with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-high`.
frictions.resolution: Fell back to direct in-session API and document validation, consistent with the mission guidance and the existing helper-launch limitation note in `.factory/library/user-testing.md`.

frictions.description: `GET /actuator/health` still reported `503 DOWN` while the auth API on `8081` was usable.
frictions.resolution: Used the target auth/admin endpoints themselves as the primary runtime evidence instead of treating the degraded actuator response as a blocker.

summary: Validated all six Lane 02 API-surface assertions directly in-session after the delegated flow-validator launch failed. The compose-backed runtime rejected onboarding when credential delivery was disabled, successful onboarding and support reset stayed free of temporary-password leakage, must-change-password sessions stayed inside the documented corridor, a representative support-reset security event revoked pre-existing bearer and refresh tokens, tenant-admin foreign-target incident-response probes stayed masked while super-admin actions still succeeded, and the retired recovery alias matched the published OpenAPI/frontend-support contract.
